rationale: The citing paper uses Spiller et al. (2013) in the data-analysis section to justify and implement Johnson-Neyman "floodlight" probing of an interaction effect in Experiment 5. This is a direct methodological role: the authors report the moderator value at which the effect becomes significant and interpret regions of significance using that approach. Even though the citation is brief, it informs how a key moderation result is tested and presented, rather than serving as generic background.
relation_description: The citing paper applies the floodlight/Johnson-Neyman probing approach from Spiller et al. (2013) to analyze its moderation findings, specifically to identify the involvement threshold where process-transfer effects become significant.
